question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: doAddEntityId()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction where `payerAccountId` is spoofed to match a victim's account ID, and then `setEntityNftTransactionPredicate()` is called with a predicate that would normally include the victim, but the exclusion `predicate.and(entityId -> !payerAccountId.equals(entityId))` at line 160 now excludes the victim's account from NFT entity transaction tracking, causing the victim's NFT transactions to be silently dropped from the entity transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: doAddEntityId()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction where the `entityTransactionBuilder` is initialized once (line 172-177) with a specific `transactionType` and `result`, and then subsequent calls to `doAddEntityId()` reuse the same builder for different entity IDs, causing all entity transactions to share the same `consensusTimestamp`, `payerAccountId`, `result`, and `type` even if those values were derived from a tampered transaction body?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getTransactionStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a `TransactionRecord` with an arbitrary `receipt.statusValue` integer (including values not in the `ResponseCodeEnum` enum), causing `getTransactionStatus()` to return that raw integer value, which is then stored as the `result` in `EntityTransaction` records, corrupting the transaction result history with invalid status codes?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: isChild()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a `TransactionRecord` with a `parentConsensusTimestamp` set to an arbitrary timestamp that does not correspond to any real parent transaction, causing `isChild()` to return `true` and the transaction to be treated as a child, while `parseParent()` fails to find a matching parent and falls back to `this.parent` (which may be null), resulting in a child transaction with no parent reference being processed with incorrect success inheritance?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: isInvalidIdError()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a `TransactionRecord` with receipt status `INVALID_ACCOUNT_ID` to cause `isInvalidIdError()` to return `true`, suppressing the `handleRecoverableError` log in `EntityRecordItemListener.onItem()` at line 99-103, thereby silently hiding what would otherwise be a logged anomaly for a transaction with an invalid entity ID, masking potential data integrity issues?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: nextHookContext()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a child transaction with `hookExecutionQueue == null` and a tampered `parent` reference pointing to a `RecordItem` with an attacker-controlled `hookExecutionQueue`, causing `nextHookContext()` to delegate to the attacker-controlled parent's queue and return arbitrary `AbstractHook.Id` values, leading to hook storage changes being attributed to wrong hook IDs and owner IDs?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: nextHookContext()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a deeply nested parent chain where each `RecordItem` has `hookExecutionQueue == null`, causing `nextHookContext()` to recursively call `parent.nextHookContext()` up the chain, potentially causing a stack overflow if the chain is deep enough, resulting in a denial-of-service during hook storage change processing?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getAndIncrementLogIndex()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger concurrent processing of the same `RecordItem` (since `logIndex` is `@NonFinal @Setter` and the null check at line 193-195 is not atomic), causing a race condition where two threads both see `logIndex == null` and both initialize it to `new AtomicInteger(0)`, resulting in duplicate log index 0 being assigned to two different contract logs, corrupting the log index sequence?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: build()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a `TransactionRecord` with a `consensusTimestamp` that overflows when converted by `DomainUtils.timestampInNanosMax()` (line 305), causing `consensusTimestamp` to be set to `Long.MAX_VALUE` or a negative value, which then corrupts all downstream timestamp-based lookups and ordering in the database?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: build()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a `TransactionRecord` whose `transactionID` differs from the `transactionBody`'s `transactionID` (since `payerAccountId` is taken from `transactionBody` at line 308 while `consensusTimestamp` is taken from `transactionRecord` at line 305), causing a mismatch between the stored payer and the stored timestamp, corrupting the transaction identity in the database?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: parseTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a `SignedTransaction` where `bodyBytes` is valid protobuf but encodes a `TransactionBody` with a different `transactionID` than what the consensus node recorded in the `TransactionRecord`, exploiting the fact that `parseTransaction()` never validates that `transactionBody.getTransactionID()` matches `transactionRecord.getTransactionID()`, causing the mirror node to store a transaction with mismatched IDs?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: parseTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a `Transaction` where `signedTransactionBytes` is non-empty but parses to a `SignedTransaction` with empty `bodyBytes`, causing `TransactionBody.parseFrom(ByteString.EMPTY)` to return a default empty `TransactionBody`, which then passes the null check at line 430 but has no `transactionID`, causing `EntityId.of(transactionBody.getTransactionID().getAccountID())` to return `EntityId.EMPTY` as the payer?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: parseTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a `Transaction` with a `signedTransactionBytes` that is valid protobuf but contains a `SignedTransaction` with a `sigMap` that has zero signature pairs, causing the mirror node to store a transaction with an empty `signatureMap`, which then causes `insertTransactionSignatures()` in `EntityRecordItemListener` to insert no signatures, making the transaction appear unsigned in the database?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: parseTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a `Transaction` with a `signedTransactionBytes` containing a `SignedTransaction` whose `sigMap` has thousands of `SignaturePair` entries, causing the mirror node to attempt to insert thousands of `TransactionSignature` records per transaction, leading to database bloat and potential denial-of-service through resource exhaustion?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: parseTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a `Transaction` where `signedTransactionBytes` contains a `SignedTransaction` with a `bodyBytes` that is valid protobuf but encodes a `TransactionBody` with a `memo` field of maximum allowed size (100 bytes), and the `TransactionRecord` has a different `memoBytes`, causing the mirror node to store the tampered memo from the body rather than the consensus-verified memo from the record?
